title: Workshop Cashier POS Application - Frontend Implementation

requirements.summary: Build a Shop Settings page where the owner can upload/change the shop logo, edit the shop name, address, phone number, and the thank-you message that appears on printed receipts
acceptanceCriteria: Settings form contains fields for: logo image upload, shop name, address, phone number, and thank-you message
Saving settings updates the backend and shows a success confirmation
Uploaded logo is displayed in the settings page and on printed receipts

file_operations.description: Create the Shop Settings page with a form containing fields for logo upload (using ExternalBlob from blob-storage component), shop name, address, phone number, and thank-you message. Wire the form to call backend methods updateShopSettings() and uploadLogo(). Display the generated default-shop-logo.dim_300x300.png from frontend/public/assets/generated/default-shop-logo.dim_300x300.png as placeholder. Show success confirmation after save. Verify the blob-storage component's usage instructions before implementing logo upload.
Add route for /settings pointing to SettingsPage component and include it in the sidebar navigation

requirements.summary: Build a Catalog Management page to manage services and spare parts (products) with add, edit, and delete operations
acceptanceCriteria: Catalog list shows all items with name, category, price, and current stock
Adding an item saves it to the backend and refreshes the list
Editing an item updates name, price, and stock in the backend
Deleting an item removes it from the catalog
Stock is decremented automatically when an item is sold in a transaction

file_operations.description: Create the Catalog Management page that displays a list of all products and services fetched via getAllProducts() and getAllServices(). Include add/edit/delete functionality calling addProduct(), addService(), deleteProduct(), deleteService(), and updateProductStock(). Show category, name, price, and stock for each item. Provide modal or inline forms for adding and editing items.
Add route for /catalog pointing to CatalogPage component and include it in the sidebar navigation

requirements.summary: Build a Cashier / New Transaction page where the cashier can search and add services or products to a cart, set quantities, apply discount, record customer and vehicle info, and finalize the transaction
acceptanceCriteria: Cashier can search catalog by name and add items to cart
Cart shows item name, quantity, unit price, and line subtotal
Grand total is computed in real-time including any discount
Cashier can enter customer name and vehicle info (plate number, vehicle type)
Completing the transaction saves it to the backend and clears the cart
After completion, the receipt print dialog is automatically triggered

file_operations.description: Create the Cashier/New Transaction page with a search bar for catalog items (fetched via getAllProducts() and getAllServices()), a cart component showing selected items with quantities and line subtotals, a grand total display with optional discount input, and fields for customer name and vehicle info. On transaction completion, call createTransaction() with the cart items, then trigger the receipt print dialog automatically via window.print() and clear the cart. Fetch customer suggestions via getAllCustomers().

Create a Receipt component that displays shop logo (from getShopSettings() or the generated default-shop-logo.dim_300x300.png from frontend/public/assets/generated/default-shop-logo.dim_300x300.png), shop name, address, phone number, transaction date/time, transaction ID, itemized list of products/services with quantities and prices, grand total, and the thank-you message. This component is used for printing via window.print().
Add route for /cashier pointing to CashierPage component and include it in the sidebar navigation as the primary entry point

requirements.summary: Implement a print-friendly receipt component that uses window.print() and works with Nativefier/Node.js desktop wrapping
acceptanceCriteria: A styled receipt component renders all required fields
A CSS print stylesheet hides navigation and non-receipt UI, showing only the receipt
Calling window.print() from the app produces a clean, well-formatted receipt
Receipt can also be triggered from the transaction history view for reprinting

file_operations.description: Enhance the Receipt component to be print-friendly, displaying all required fields: shop logo from getShopSettings() (or the generated default-shop-logo.dim_300x300.png from frontend/public/assets/generated/default-shop-logo.dim_300x300.png as fallback), shop details, transaction data, itemized list, total, and thank-you message. Ensure it is styled for clarity when printed.

Add a CSS @media print block that hides navigation, sidebar, and all non-receipt UI elements, ensuring only the Receipt component is visible during window.print(). Style the receipt for clean paper output.

requirements.summary: Build a Transaction History page that lists all past transactions with reprint functionality and date range filtering
acceptanceCriteria: Transaction list is paginated or scrollable and shows key transaction details
Clicking a transaction opens its full detail/receipt view
Reprint button on each transaction triggers window.print() for that receipt
Date range filter narrows the list to the selected period

file_operations.description: Create the Transaction History page that fetches and displays all transactions via getAllTransactions(). Show transaction date, customer name, vehicle info, and total amount. Include a date range filter to narrow results using getTransactionsByMonth() or client-side filtering. Each transaction row has a reprint button that loads the transaction detail via getTransaction() and triggers window.print() with the Receipt component.
Add route for /transactions pointing to TransactionHistoryPage component and include it in the sidebar navigation

requirements.summary: Build a Reports page showing daily and monthly sales summaries with date/month picker navigation
acceptanceCriteria: Daily report shows total revenue and transaction count for a selected date
Monthly report shows total revenue and transaction count for a selected month
Top-selling items (by quantity sold) are listed for the selected period
Reports data is fetched from the backend actor

file_operations.description: Create the Reports page with date and month pickers. Fetch daily reports via getDailyReport() and monthly reports via getMonthlyReport(). Display total revenue, transaction count, and top-selling items using getTopSellingItems(). Allow the user to switch between daily and monthly views.
Add route for /reports pointing to ReportsPage component and include it in the sidebar navigation

requirements.summary: Design and apply a cohesive visual theme: dark navy and orange-amber colors, bold industrial typography, card-based layouts, and a persistent sidebar navigation
acceptanceCriteria: All pages share the same color palette, typography, and component style
Sidebar navigation is always visible on desktop with clear active-state highlighting
App feels polished and purpose-built for a workshop environment

file_operations.description: Update global styles to define the color palette (dark navy primary, orange-amber accent), bold industrial typography (e.g., 'Roboto Condensed', 'Inter', or similar sans-serif), and card-based layout styling. Ensure consistency across all pages.

Extend the Tailwind theme to include custom colors (dark navy, orange-amber) and typography settings that align with the industrial workshop aesthetic. Configure card, button, and navigation component styles.

Create a Sidebar component with navigation links (icons and labels) for Cashier, Catalog, Transactions, Reports, and Settings. Apply active-state highlighting using the industrial theme colors. Display the generated bengkel-logo.dim_256x256.png from frontend/public/assets/generated/bengkel-logo.dim_256x256.png at the top of the sidebar.
Integrate the Sidebar component into the main App layout so it is persistently visible on all pages. Ensure the layout accommodates the sidebar and main content area.
